wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Mulah project overview section focused on the frontend component library demonstration. Explain its purpose as a React-based table component showcase, architecture overview, and relationship to the broader React ecosystem. Document the implementation approach using TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and modern React patterns. Include both conceptual overviews for beginners understanding component libraries and technical details for experienced developers. Use terminology consistent with the React and component library ecosystem. Provide practical examples demonstrating the table component system and mathematical calculation demonstrations. Document the project's educational value and real-world applicability for React developers.

wiki_catalogs.name: Table Container Component
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Table container component focused on the main table wrapper implementation. Explain how the Table component serves as the primary container with scrollable functionality using overflow-x-auto. Document the component's props interface including className parameter and React.ComponentProps inheritance. Detail the data-slot attribute usage for accessibility and styling hooks. Explain the relationship between the Table component and its child components through the data-slot system. Include practical examples showing how to use the Table component as a wrapper for complete table structures. Address responsive design considerations and scroll behavior on different screen sizes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started content for the Mulah project focused on quick setup and initial usage. Explain installation requirements including Node.js version, dependency installation via npm/yarn, and development server startup. Document the build process, available scripts, and development workflow. Include step-by-step instructions for running the project locally, understanding the project structure, and exploring the table component demonstrations. Provide guidance on modifying the table components, adding new calculations, and customizing styling. Address common setup issues and troubleshooting tips for beginners. Make the content accessible to developers new to React component libraries while providing clear next steps for deeper exploration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Table Structure Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for table structure components including TableHeader, TableBody, and TableFooter components. Explain how these components organize table sections with proper semantic HTML structure. Document the styling patterns used for borders, shadows, and visual separation between sections. Detail the data-slot attributes and their role in the overall table architecture. Include examples showing proper composition of table sections and how they work together to create complete table structures. Address styling variations and customization options for different table designs.

wiki_catalogs.name: Technology Stack & Dependencies
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive technology stack documentation for the Mulah project focused on the complete development environment and toolchain. Explain React 19.2.4 as the core framework, TypeScript integration for type safety, and Vite 8.0.1 as the build tool. Document Tailwind CSS 4.2.2 for utility-first styling, Base UI 1.3.0 for accessible primitives, and Lucide React 1.6.0 for iconography. Detail the development tools including ESLint configuration, build optimization, and modern JavaScript features. Include dependency management, version compatibility, and upgrade considerations. Provide technical insights into why these technologies were chosen and how they work together to create an optimal development experience for component libraries.

wiki_catalogs.name: Table Row and Cell Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for TableRow, TableHead, and TableCell components focused on individual table elements and their interactions. Explain the row-level styling including hover effects, selection states, and transition behaviors. Document cell-specific styling patterns for data presentation, alignment, and spacing. Detail the checkbox integration patterns and responsive design considerations. Include examples showing how to create interactive tables with selectable rows and sortable columns. Address accessibility features and keyboard navigation support.

wiki_catalogs.name: Table Component System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the comprehensive table component system focused on the complete table architecture and implementation. Explain the component hierarchy including Table (main container), TableHeader, TableBody, TableRow, TableCell, TableHead, and TableCaption components. Document the component composition patterns, prop interfaces, and TypeScript type definitions. Include practical examples showing how components work together to create functional tables with mathematical calculations. Detail the styling approach using Tailwind CSS classes, responsive design considerations, and accessibility features. Provide usage patterns for different table scenarios, customization options, and integration with external data sources. Address performance considerations and best practices for large datasets.

wiki_catalogs.name: Table Caption Component
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create documentation for the TableCaption component focused on table labeling and descriptive text placement. Explain the component's role in providing context and accessibility information for tables. Document the styling approach including positioning, typography, and responsive behavior. Include examples showing proper usage of captions for different table types and data presentations. Address accessibility considerations and screen reader compatibility for table descriptions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Utility Systems
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the utility systems focused on the cn() function and class merging utilities. Explain the implementation of the cn() function that combines Tailwind CSS classes using clsx and tailwind-merge for conflict resolution. Document the utility's role in the component system, performance benefits, and usage patterns throughout the table components. Include practical examples showing how to use the utility function for dynamic styling, conditional classes, and theme customization. Detail the integration with Tailwind CSS JIT compilation and how the utility maintains clean, conflict-free class combinations. Provide guidelines for extending the utility system and best practices for component styling.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Processing & Mathematical Calculations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the data processing and mathematical calculation system focused on the demonstration of arithmetic operations within the table components. Explain the calculation logic including alpha (addition: A5 + A20), beta (division: A15 / A7), and charlie (multiplication: A13 * A12). Document the data structure management, variable declarations, and how calculations integrate with the table rendering system. Include examples showing how raw data arrays are processed, calculated results are displayed, and the relationship between data flow and component rendering. Detail the TypeScript interfaces for data objects, error handling for mathematical operations, and best practices for data integrity. Provide guidance on adapting the calculation system for different data sources and business logic.

wiki_catalogs.name: Styling & Design System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive styling and design system documentation focused on the Tailwind CSS integration and component styling patterns. Explain the utility-first approach, responsive design implementation, and accessibility considerations throughout the table components. Document the design tokens, color schemes, spacing systems, and typography usage. Include examples of custom styling, theme customization, and component variant creation. Detail the integration between the cn() utility function and Tailwind classes, hover effects, and interactive states. Provide guidelines for maintaining design consistency, extending the design system, and creating new component variants. Address performance optimization through efficient class usage and CSS generation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Build & Development Setup
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive build and development setup documentation focused on the Vite configuration, TypeScript setup, and development workflow. Explain the Vite build configuration including plugins, optimization settings, and development server configuration. Document the TypeScript configuration files, path aliases, and type checking setup. Include guidance on environment variables, build optimization, and production deployment preparation. Detail the development workflow, hot module replacement, and debugging techniques. Provide troubleshooting guides for common build issues, performance optimization tips, and best practices for maintaining the development environment. Address version compatibility and upgrade considerations for the build toolchain.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# Mulah Project Overview

## 1. Project Introduction

### Purpose Statement
Mulah is a React-based web application designed to demonstrate data visualization through interactive tables. The project showcases mathematical calculations and data presentation in a clean, modern interface using contemporary frontend technologies.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- Demonstrate React component architecture with TypeScript
- Showcase Tailwind CSS styling integration with custom utility functions
- Implement reusable UI components following design system principles
- Display calculated results in organized table formats
- Provide a foundation for future data visualization enhancements

### Target Audience
- Frontend developers learning React and modern JavaScript frameworks
- Students studying data visualization techniques
- Developers exploring component-based UI architectures
- Anyone interested in practical examples of React + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS integration

## 2. Technical Architecture

### Component Breakdown
The project follows a modular React architecture with clear separation of concerns:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[main.tsx] --> B[App.tsx]
    B --> C[components/ui/table.tsx]
    C --> D[lib/utils.ts]
    A --> E[index.html]
    B --> F[App.tsx]
    F --> G[Table Components]
    G --> H[Table]
    G --> I[TableHeader]
    G --> J[TableBody]
    G --> K[TableRow]
    G --> L[TableCell]
    G --> M[TableCaption]
```

### Design Patterns
- **Component Composition Pattern**: UI components are built from smaller, reusable pieces
- **Utility-First Styling**: Custom `cn()` function combines Tailwind classes with conditional logic
- **Configuration-Driven Development**: Vite and ESLint configurations provide consistent development experience
- **TypeScript Integration**: Strong typing throughout the application for better developer experience

### System Relationships
```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[React Application] --> B[UI Components]
    B --> C[Styling System]
    C --> D[Build Tools]
    
    B --> E[table.tsx]
    E --> F[utils.ts]
    
    D --> G[Vite Config]
    D --> H[ESLint Config]
    
    G --> I[Development Server]
    H --> J[Code Quality]
```

### Data Flow
```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant User
    participant App
    participant Table
    participant Utils
    
    User->>App: Load Application
    App->>App: Initialize Variables
    App->>Table: Render Data Tables
    Table->>Utils: Apply Styling Classes
    Utils-->>Table: Combined CSS Classes
    Table-->>User: Display Styled Tables
```

## 3. Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points
- **Application Entry**: `src/main.tsx` - Bootstraps the React application and renders the root component
- **Root Component**: `src/App.tsx` - Contains the main application logic and data structures
- **HTML Template**: `index.html` - Provides the basic HTML structure with React root element

### Core Modules
- **UI Components**: `src/components/ui/table.tsx` - Implements a comprehensive table component system with multiple sub-components
- **Utility Functions**: `src/lib/utils.ts` - Provides the `cn()` function for merging Tailwind CSS classes
- **Build Configuration**: `vite.config.ts` - Configures Vite build tool with React and Tailwind CSS plugins
- **Code Quality**: `eslint.config.js` - Defines ESLint rules for TypeScript and React development

### Configuration Approach
- **Package Management**: `package.json` - Manages dependencies including React, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and development tools
- **Alias Resolution**: Vite configuration provides `@` alias for cleaner imports
- **Plugin Integration**: React and Tailwind CSS plugins streamline development workflow

### External Dependencies
Key dependencies include:
- **React & ReactDOM**: Core framework for building user interfaces
- **Tailwind CSS**: Utility-first CSS framework for styling
- **Base UI**: Component library providing accessible UI primitives
- **Lucide React**: Icon library for consistent visual elements
- **TypeScript**: Type safety and enhanced development experience

### Integration Points
- **Component Communication**: Parent-child relationships between App and Table components
- **Styling Integration**: Direct CSS class application through the `cn()` utility function
- **Build Tooling**: Vite handles bundling, development server, and production builds
- **Code Quality**: ESLint enforces coding standards and best practices

### Component Relationship Diagram
```mermaid
graph LR
    A[App.tsx] --> B[Table Components]
    B --> C[Table]
    B --> D[TableHeader]
    B --> E[TableBody]
    B --> F[TableRow]
    B --> G[TableCell]
    
    H[utils.ts] --> I[cn() function]
    I --> B
    
    J[main.tsx] --> A
    K[vite.config.ts] --> L[Build Process]
    M[eslint.config.js] --> N[Code Quality]
```

## 4. Key Features

### Functionality Overview
- **Data Presentation**: Displays raw data and calculated results in organized table formats
- **Mathematical Calculations**: Performs basic arithmetic operations (addition, division, multiplication)
- **Interactive Tables**: Provides scrollable table containers for responsive data display
- **Modern Styling**: Implements contemporary design principles with Tailwind CSS

### Implementation Highlights
- **Reusable Table System**: Modular table components that can be composed for different use cases
- **Flexible Styling**: Utility-first approach allows for easy customization and theming
- **Type Safety**: Full TypeScript support ensures reliable code throughout the application
- **Performance Optimization**: Lightweight implementation focused on essential functionality

### Feature Architecture
```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> DataInitialization
    DataInitialization --> TableRendering
    TableRendering --> CalculationProcessing
    CalculationProcessing --> ResultDisplay
    ResultDisplay --> [*]
    
    TableRendering --> StylingApplication
    StylingApplication --> ResultDisplay
```

The application demonstrates a clean separation between data processing, calculation logic, and presentation layers, making it an excellent example of modern React development practices.

---

**Sources:**
- [package.json](package.json)
- [App.tsx](src/App.tsx)
- [table.tsx](src/components/ui/table.tsx)
- [utils.ts](src/lib/utils.ts)
- [vite.config.ts](vite.config.ts)
- [eslint.config.js](eslint.config.js)
- [index.html](index.html)
- [main.tsx](src/main.tsx)

</blog>
